name: stop overthinking
desc: We’ve all been there: the endless loops of I should have said... or What if they think... Overthinking feels like a heavy fog that keeps us from seeing the beauty of the present. Stop Overthinking is more than just a self-help book; it is a gentle invitation to let go of the need for absolute certainty. Through relatable examples and science-backed psychology, it teaches us that peace isn't the absence of thoughts—it’s the ability to choose which ones we listen to.If you’re tired of feeling exhausted by your own mind, this book is the deep breath you’ve been waiting for

name: How to Finish What You Start
desc:
How many times have you started a project with great enthusiasm, only to find it abandoned in the corner of forgetfulness? The gap between starting and finishing is where our most important dreams and goals get lost.

The book How to Finish What You Start is your practical guide to closing that gap. It dives into the psychology of follow-through and achievement, analyzing the hidden reasons behind laziness and loss of passion, while providing concrete strategies to build unshakable self-discipline.

If you are tired of unfinished projects and want to turn your ideas into reality, this book is the engine you need to reach the finish line
